image_dependency: requires_image=true indicates the question is intended to rely on an image
text_dominant=false suggests the key evidence is not primarily recoverable from text alone
the question asks about rivers flowing eastward toward the Baltic Sea, which typically requires reading spatial/map information from the image
asset_registry_record shows one existing image with high readability_score=0.901, so the visual dependency is supported by available assets
multi_step: answering likely requires identifying a set of rivers by their eastward flow direction and destination (Baltic Sea), then mapping that set to a river type label
this is more than direct visual lookup because it combines spatial direction with geographic classification
the item is still short and open-ended with no explicit calculation or long chain of constraints, so multi-step demand appears moderate rather than high
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text is a concise category label ('Consequent rivers'), which is straightforward to compare against expected output
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true support reliable downstream checking
because the expected answer is a named geography term, exact-match or normalized-match verification is feasible
verifiability is not maximal because short-text answers can have synonym or capitalization variation
